TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Kavalan, story: KavCon-earthquake_abas_haciang

S.FORM: yau iku tazian ayi tamaisuan tina haw.
S.TRANSL: 我在阿姨你這邊。
I’m here at your place, my dear aunt.

S.FORM: haw.
S.TRANSL: 嗯。
Yeah.

S.FORM: mawtu iku timaisuan.
S.TRANSL: 我來到你家。
I came to your place.

S.FORM: ngid iku ipil tu masang ay a zau utuz zinna nani.
S.TRANSL: 他(某位台大學生)說「我想要聽聽有關從前地震的事」。
He (an NTU student) said, ‘I want to hear something about the earthquake in the past.’

S.FORM: naquni wawayta nazau na utuz tazian ta pakteRungan zinku timaisu nani.
S.TRANSL: 我跟你說，「新社這裡的地震情形是如何」。
I said to you, ‘How was the earthquake here in PakteRung?’

S.FORM: naquni a aisu m qaqaqa supaR tu zau tu utuz zinku.
S.TRANSL: 我想你不管如何年紀都比我長，應該知道有關地震的事。
I think that since you’re older than me, anyhow you’d know something about the earthquake.

S.FORM: nayau sayzi Raynguanku qaya utuz ni azu.
S.TRANSL: 那樣也許沒錯，但是我並不知道什麼有關地震的事情，好像...
That might be the case, but I know nothing about the earthquake, like..

S.FORM: eh m Raynguanku qaya yu.
S.TRANSL: 我也不知道耶。
I don’t know, either.

S.FORM: pasyani niqasianemanku Raymazuq ay zinku a.
S.TRANSL: 我也不知道往哪裡想，我想我很笨。
My thought wanders around, and I think I’m stupid.

S.FORM: u qawanay ti aisu supaR ay msanem ay nani.
S.TRANSL: 所以應該由比較知道而且也比較聰明的你(來說地震的故事)。
So (the one who can tell us the story) should be you, who are both knowledgeable and smart.

S.FORM: qametansu snikawmaanna a zana utuz a yau.
S.TRANSL: 你記得他說的，那個地震的(事情)。
You remember what he said about that earthquake.

S.FORM: Raynguanku aikuan.
S.TRANSL: 我真的不知道。
I really don’t know.

S.FORM: aisu pasikawma ya.
S.TRANSL: 你來說吧。
(Come on), tell us the story.

S.FORM: nayau ya naquni a qeni utuzanta naquni qenasupaRansu zinku ya.
S.TRANSL: 那樣啊，不過我還是覺得你應該是知道我們以前地震的事。
Well, but I still somehow think that you know something about the earthquake that happened to us in the past.

S.FORM: nani yau pama aimi tatebaRungan.
S.TRANSL: 嗯, (那是發生在)我們還住在TebaRung的時候。
Well, (that happened when) we still lived in TebaRung.

S.FORM: yau mutuz nani yau imi qman sataRbabi nani.
S.TRANSL: 我們那時候正在吃早餐，然後地震就來了。
When we were having breakfast, the earthquake happened.

S.FORM: yau ti utuz a yau nianpa nizanna bibiaq na utuz a kaysin.
S.TRANSL: 地震就來啦，然後地震就把所有的碗都震倒了，真是可惜阿。
The earthquake happened. It’s a pity that all the bowls fell off due to the earthquake.

S.FORM: m ah nayau a utuz a yau masang.
S.TRANSL: 從前的地震就是那樣。
The earthquake in the past is like that.

S.FORM: qaytisan qaya haw.
S.TRANSL: 很危險。
(It’s) dangerous.

S.FORM: m nani unay ti kwa masesebang ti kwa peliyas tawian tap tapungkangan.
S.TRANSL: 洞穴那邊的懸崖很多都塌了。
Many cliffs near the cave collapsed.

S.FORM: m nayau kwa utuz a yau nani.
S.TRANSL: 那時的地震就是那個樣子。
The earthquake was like that.

S.FORM: sinapawan ti isu unay.
S.TRANSL: 那時你結婚了沒?
Were you married at that time?

S.FORM: yau ti.
S.TRANSL: 結了。
I was.

S.FORM: yau ti haw.
S.TRANSL: 結了嗎？
Were you?

S.FORM: sinapawan ti na mai pama sasunis.
S.TRANSL: 結婚了，但是還沒生小孩。
(I) was married, but hadn’t had any children yet.

S.FORM: yau pama imi tatebalonan yu ‘nay Raya utuz.
S.TRANSL: 大地震那時，我們還在tebalon那裡。
We were still at Tebalon when the strong earthquake happened.

S.FORM: aiku nani sepaRanku ya masang nayau a mutuz tu Raya ‘nay nani kiya pama iku.
S.TRANSL: 我知道以前那個大地震時，我還很小。
I was still very little when I knew the strong earthquake happened.

S.FORM: Rabtin pama tasawku unay nani.
S.TRANSL: 我那時年紀才十歲。
I was only ten years old then.

S.FORM: utulu betin yau usiwa tasaw a ‘nay .
S.TRANSL: 那時是(民國)三十九年。

S.FORM: nayau azu sanusu smuRap ya utuz a yau.
S.TRANSL: 正如你說的那樣，地震是(發生)在早上。
Like what you said, the earthquake (happened) in the early morning.

S.FORM: smuRap saizi qman pama ita sataRbabi.
S.TRANSL: 是早上沒錯，(因為)我們(那時候)還在吃早餐。
It was in the early morning. We were still eating breakfast.

S.FORM: utuz a yau baqiku matiw sa lazing putiamling miza ya Raybaut aizipna tikinzay.
S.TRANSL: 我阿公去海邊放網捕魚
My grandpa went to the sea to net and catch fish.

S.FORM: m kinawsa nayau.
S.TRANSL: 他們，和sinzay兩個人。
They two ... together with Sinzay.

S.FORM: seqawalu qumni unay mana Raynguanku ka.
S.TRANSL: 那是夏天還是什麼時候？我怎麼不記得了？
Was it summer, or sometime else? How come I can’t remember?

S.FORM: seqawalu seqawalu yu.
S.TRANSL: 是夏天。
It was summer.

S.FORM: seqawalu haw.
S.TRANSL: 是夏天對吧？
It was summer, wasn’t it?

S.FORM: m nani seqawalu.
S.TRANSL: 是夏天。
It was summer.

S.FORM: tuRabi ya wi nani taRbabi suna su sudat pa tu salilna nani.
S.TRANSL: 那個晚上去，早上收網。
(They) left at night and retrieved their net in the morning.

S.FORM: kinawsa ti kinzay yau a ipazna tu e iza u ‘nay ‘nay qiwiqiw ay a pelunana.
S.TRANSL: 他們左右划槳。
They roared left and right.

S.FORM: ipaz masang.
S.TRANSL: 以前的竹筏就是那樣。
The rafts in the past were like that.

S.FORM: n ya tu mana smani qaya mutuz unay nani.
S.TRANSL: 也不知道那個地震...
Not knowing the earthquake...

S.FORM: wi iku pasazui ta ngayauan na qani iza u na lepawan na qaniisaku tayan kwa taqawRian.
S.TRANSL: 我轉到isaku他們以前的家的前面，靠海的那個地方。
I turned toward the front of the house where Isaku once lived, toward the place near the sea.

S.FORM: nani maqayta ya betu a yau Raya ay.
S.TRANSL: 看到那個大石頭。
(I) saw the the big rock.

S.FORM: yau ti imu tayan ya yau pama imi ta mutuz.
S.TRANSL: 地震那時，你們已經在那邊了嗎?
Had you been there when the earthquake happened?

S.FORM: yau pama imi ta tayan ta zana kyukay tangi ta zana e taqsian.
S.TRANSL: 我們還在現在教會的那個地方，在學校那邊。
We were still at the place where the church is located now, near the school.

S.FORM: zinku ya taqsian ni liangu.
S.TRANSL: 學校，就在現在liangu住的那邊。
School, beside (the place where) liangu lives now, I think.

S.FORM: na usa usa.
S.TRANSL: 不不不。
No, no, no.

S.FORM: tazian pama imi ta izaan u tazana taqsian tatuqeban na taqsian tangi.
S.TRANSL: 我們還在那個...那個...現在的學校的後面。
We were still at the ... the rear of the school.

S.FORM: takyukay tangi ta qani tina bayi tipilawan tuqeban.
S.TRANSL: 在現在的教會，Pilaw阿姨家的後面。
(At the place where) the church is now. At the rear of Pilaw’s (house).

S.FORM: o qaniiza qaya qaniahun atu ti iza kwa qanialan ti aswan.
S.TRANSL: 也是在那個Ahun還有那個Alan和Asuan (家的後面)。
Also (at the rear of) ... Ahun’s, and ... Alan’s and Aswan’s.

S.FORM: e yau bayi tipaytawku.
S.TRANSL: 還有Paytawku (的家)。
And of Paytawku’s.

S.FORM: n yau pama tayan na lepawna haw.
S.TRANSL: 對，他們的房子(那時)還在那邊。
Yes, their houses were still there (then).

S.FORM: lepawna qanipaytawku yau a bai tiRyanga maq ilan ‘nay qani bai tiRyanga kya kebalan qaya.
S.TRANSL: 那個Paytawku阿媽也在那邊，還有Ryanga，那個從宜蘭來的噶瑪蘭人。
Paytawku, a female elder, was there as well. And also Ryanga, who came from Ilan.

S.FORM: masang msusupa ita nani.
S.TRANSL: 以前我們很窮。
We were very poor in the past.

S.FORM: o Haciang zinna iku zau ‘may zin na baibudan a yau.
S.TRANSL: 那個阿媽會跟我說：「喔！Raciang，這是(要給你的)飯」。
The female elder would say to me, ‘Oh, Haciang, this is rice (for you).’

S.FORM: nayau ni sikawmaanna ya nengi nitangkebalananna mai tu.
S.TRANSL: 她那樣(跟我說)，她噶瑪蘭語講的很好。
(She told me) in that way, and she spoke Kavalan well.

S.FORM: m zuma ya zana ilan na sikawmaan.
S.TRANSL: 宜蘭那邊的話不一樣。
The language (spoken) in Ilan is different.

S.FORM: tu taRbabi unay nani.
S.TRANSL: 那個早上，
In that morning,

S.FORM: nazau sunis pama ita m duna smasaRis kya.
S.TRANSL: 我們還是小孩時，常常亂跑，不是嗎？
When we were still children, we often went around, didn’t we?

S.FORM: wiya ti iku suaRi nani.
S.TRANSL: 我往海邊方向走去。
I went toward the sea.

S.FORM: timu mutuz tu snayau a Raya ya utuz a yau utuz.
S.TRANSL: 結果卻發生了大地震。
It turned out that the strong earthquake took palce.

S.FORM: m‘eted ti ya zing azau ti snayau a m niana zinta ti.
S.TRANSL: 海就乾掉了，好像那樣，嗯 ... 我們該怎麼說？
The sea dried up, much like ... how shall we put it?

S.FORM: miRemut ya zing nani limzusa yu.
S.TRANSL: 海分成兩半。
The sea was separated into two parts.

S.FORM: tawian tabetuan ‘nay Raya ay betu ‘nay siangRay anu zinta tangi.
S.TRANSL: 就在大石頭那個地方，就是現在我們叫SiangRay那邊。
Out there, where the huge rock is located. We now call it siangRay.

S.FORM: maizaan mutuz nani mabenaR haw.
S.TRANSL: 地震的時候，(海)就裂開了。
When the earthquake happened, (the sea) split.

S.FORM: en ya mai ti tu utuz nani.
S.TRANSL: 地震停了後，
After the earthquake stopped,

S.FORM: tmuqaz ti zanum nani zazamanna ti ita.
S.TRANSL: 水又回來趕上我們。
The water went back and caught up with us.

S.FORM: en ya nayau haw masang baqiku haw.
S.TRANSL: 沒錯，以前我的阿公就是那個樣子。
Indeed, in the past my grandpa was just like that.

S.FORM: mqaipaz tmuzus tayan ta beteb na Raya ay betu nani.
S.TRANSL: (他)搭竹筏到一大石頭那裡。
(He) took a raft and reached there near the huge rock.

S.FORM: pameng ti maqatedan nani.
S.TRANSL: 海(的中間)就乾掉了。
The sea dried up (in the middle).

S.FORM: qatedan ti haw.
S.TRANSL: 就乾掉了。
It dried up.

S.FORM: limzusa yu pasaqazqaz u‘siq.
S.TRANSL: (海)被分成兩半，一半往岸邊。
(The ocean) was separated into two halves, one of which moved toward the seashore.

S.FORM: pas pasa qazqaz.
S.TRANSL: 往岸邊移動。
(It) moved toward the seashore.

S.FORM: m‘eted zinna.
S.TRANSL: 據說海就乾了。
It was said that the sea dried up.

S.FORM: m‘eted en.
S.TRANSL: 是啊，就乾掉了。
Yes, it dried up.

S.FORM: mlimzusa azau ita mlyaRicay kwa.
S.TRANSL: (海水)被分成兩半，好像我們抓魚仔的時候。
It was separated into two parts, like when we were catching small fish.

S.FORM: tu Ribaut tu smuRuq ita nani yau a iza kwa.
S.TRANSL: 我們在撈魚的時候，有那個...
When we were catching fish by scooping, there was something...

S.FORM: yau a ibabaw ta tebteban nani.
S.TRANSL: 有中間高高的(海浪)。
There were (waves) that were high in the middle.

S.FORM: yau a zanum mlimzusa snayau estaR tu ‘nay maqayta buqan yu.
S.TRANSL: 水就分成兩半，好像可以看得見沙子那樣。
The water was separated into two parts, in the way like, the sand could be seen.

S.FORM: ‘nay betu ‘nay haw qawman azu baniw.
S.TRANSL: 那顆石頭(指siangRay)就像香菇，(因為水面下降)。
The rock looked like a mushroom, (because the water was going down).

S.FORM: nayau sayzi nani.
S.TRANSL: 也許就是那樣。
Perhaps it’s like that.

S.FORM: nayau qatedan ti baut nya nya qatiw pa ita ala tu baut zinna na baqian nani.
S.TRANSL: 然後魚就沒有水了，於是我祖父就跟我說：「我們去撿魚吧」。
The fish were running out of water, so my grandpa said to me, ‘Let’s go pick up the fish!’

S.FORM: wiya ti smudat yu baqiku.
S.TRANSL: 我祖父就去收網。
My grandpa went to retrieve his net.

S.FORM: tangi wa zaapanna ti na tenes zing Raya haw.
S.TRANSL: 現在，海水上漲了。
Now, the sea was flowing back.

S.FORM: tenes sadukian si.
S.TRANSL: 很慢，大概一個小時吧。
It took a long time. About an hour.

S.FORM: sudatanna ti salilna nu ninuR ti tu buqan si.
S.TRANSL: 收網的時候，他就踩在淺攤上面。
When he was retrieving his net, (he) stepped on the shallow beach.

S.FORM: mai ti mzaqis ta ipazna.
S.TRANSL: 甚至不用坐竹筏(就可以收網)。
He didn’t (even have to) take his canoe (to retrieve his net).

S.FORM: pameng ti tabuqanan haw.
S.TRANSL: 只好在沙子上。
There’s no other choice but (to stand) on the sand.

S.FORM: tebeRanna ti ya buqan niizaan nayau.
S.TRANSL: 他就踩在沙子上。
He stepped on the sand.

S.FORM: atani ti qaibabaw iRbeng nayau betu a yau qaya.
S.TRANSL: (你看)那石頭(指siangRay)有多高，多深。
How tall as well as deep that stone was!

S.FORM: tu iza qaya yu nayau mulpun ti ya miza tu salilna nani yau ti zing a yau mai qasiR paqanas.
S.TRANSL: 拿了他的網子以後，海就來了，並不快，慢慢的。
After he took his net, here came the sea, not fast, but slow.

S.FORM: azu ita ’nay meRnap ay zing kwa.
S.TRANSL: 好像我們漲潮時候的海那樣。
Much like the sea that is rising.

S.FORM: en paqanas kwa tmadikud mpuniR.
S.TRANSL: 慢慢的回去以後，又漲滿了。
It returned slowly, and then got full.

S.FORM: nani niyanpa qaya ya qudusna.
S.TRANSL: 衣服不見了。
The clothes were gone as well.

S.FORM: matiw sa waRi nani nisanna ya qelapiyanna na qaniyau na baqian.
S.TRANSL: 老人出海前會脫掉內褲。
Before going to the sea, the elders would take off their underpants.

S.FORM: en ya qaseseng ti ita zinna qaya saiza.
S.TRANSL: 他們說，我們會冷。
‘We would feel cold,’ said they.

S.FORM: nisanna tayan ta bebetuan nani wi taliqawan tangi ya.
S.TRANSL: 在石頭那邊脫衣，在現在轉彎的地方。
(They would) take off their clothes beside the stones, at the corner now.

S.FORM: m tu tmanan nani niyanpa ya mai ti tu qelapiyan tabuanna ti tu pelingid.
S.TRANSL: 回來時什麼都沒有了，內褲不見了，只好用山芋頭的葉子包住(身體)。
After they returned, their underpants were gone so they covered (their body) with taro leaves.

S.FORM: mai qmeqepeq ti tu betu a izaanna nani.
S.TRANSL: 他們沒有抓到石頭。
They didn’t grab stones.

S.FORM: mai tu betu tabuqanan si.
S.TRANSL: 在沙灘上沒有石頭。
There were no stones on the sandy beach.

S.FORM: a ya salil ‘nay salil a yau taizaan si tawaRi niz buqan.
S.TRANSL: 漁網在海中，全部都是沙子。
The net was in the sea, full of sand.

S.FORM: nani tmanan nani qmazaw ta salilna nani.
S.TRANSL: 回來後，扛漁網。
After they returned, they carried their net on the shoulder.

S.FORM: masebit ti ya niya niazazasna ya tabuanna tu pengilid.
S.TRANSL: 山芋頭葉破掉了，包在裡面的器官露出來了。
The taro leaves were torn, so their (sexual) organs (which) were wrapped in taro leaves (were exposed).

S.FORM: tanian mkala tu pengilid.
S.TRANSL: 在那裡找到山芋頭葉?
Where did they find taro leaves?

S.FORM: yau masang tayan ta iRuRan ta.
S.TRANSL: 以前那邊有。
There were (taro leaves) over there in the past.

S.FORM: taqaimisan na tazian iRuR takwa talaqayaqai zau.
S.TRANSL: 在這條河的北邊。
(There were) taro leaves to the north of the river here.

S.FORM: mlaziw tu iRuR a yau.
S.TRANSL: 他們經過這河時，(別人)大笑。
When they went along this river, (people) kept laughing.

S.FORM: mtawtawa ti ya alaanna ti na zing saiza ya qelapiyanna.
S.TRANSL: 他們的內褲是不是被海沖走了?
Their underpants might have been washed away by the sea.

S.FORM: en ya.
S.TRANSL: 對。
Yes.

S.FORM: Rayawu qudusna mniz.
S.TRANSL: (還有)全部的衣服。
(and) all their clothes.

S.FORM: qem nayau pitemawaR matiw nani nisanna tayan ta bebetuan.
S.TRANSL: 每次去的時候，把脫掉的(衣褲，放在)石頭那裡。
Every time when they went to the sea, they would take off their clothes and place them where there were stones.

S.FORM: sikedang ti matiw sa waRi nani.
S.TRANSL: 光著屁股出海。
(They) went to the sea not wearing any underpants.

S.FORM: tmanan nani awka mayseng mai ita sesen zinna.
S.TRANSL: 回來後,衣服才是乾的，我們不會冷。
After (we) came back, (the clothes) would become dry and (we would) not feel cold.

S.FORM: mai ti tu sa mai ti tawiqanta baqian niyanpa ya yau iza si mkala ti saiza tu mlalazuk ti mala ti tu punki.
S.TRANSL: 我們跟老人打招呼，可憐喔，他們急急忙忙找到畚箕來遮。
(When we) greeted the elders, they hurriedly found dust baskets (to cover their sexual organs).

S.FORM: en ya.
S.TRANSL: 對。
Yes.

S.FORM: tabuanna ti ya punuzna mniz.
S.TRANSL: 他們包住屁股。
They all covered their buttocks.

S.FORM: niyanpa imi mtawtawa ti.
S.TRANSL: 我們笑的受不了。
We kept laughing.

S.FORM: nayau saizi ya utuz masang.
S.TRANSL: 以前的地震就是那個樣子。
The earthquake in the past was like that.

S.FORM: qaqaytisan utuz masang.
S.TRANSL: 可怕的地震。
The earthquake was terrifying.

S.FORM: tenes qaya azu utani bulan azu mtenes .
S.TRANSL: 很久，好像好幾個月。
For a long time ... It also seemed that the earthquake lasted for several months. It seemed to be a long time.

S.FORM: wama ‘nay qasupaRanku haw nani ‘nay ya Raya ay ‘nay tu zana m iza u.
S.TRANSL: 只有那個我最記得。
What I remembered was that was a big (earthquake).

S.FORM: ‘nay e pun mutuz nani.
S.TRANSL: 地震結束後。
After the earthquake ended,

S.FORM: supaRanku ti tilienhuy mngayit mngayit tilienhuy ‘nay mutuz.
S.TRANSL: 我已經知道lienhui有兔唇了。
I had known that Lienhuy had harelip.

S.FORM: taRawna taRaw ‘nay.
S.TRANSL: 他的病。那個病。
His illness. The illness.

S.FORM: zamanna ti na utuz zaman na utuz iza u tipyalun na e paqan ti izaan tilienhuyan mngayit tiwi tilienhuy.
S.TRANSL: Pyalun要餵Lienhuy時，被地震趕上(遇到地震)。
The earthquake happened when Pyalun wanted to feed Lienhuy.

S.FORM: n za sunisna ni tuyaw singsi qaya nayau nayau mpatay kwa ti‘mut nanganna na sunisna mazmun ‘nay ya nayau a.
S.TRANSL: Tuyaw老師的小孩’mut也是那樣子死的，很多都是那樣子的。
Teacher Tuyaw’s child died in that way as well. His child’s name was ‘mut. Many were like that.

S.FORM: zaman na utuz a qenabinus na qani lienhuy.
S.TRANSL: Lienhuy他們出生就遇到大地震。
When Lienhuy and others were born, the earthquake happened.

S.FORM: a qenabinus na qanilienhuy ‘nay haw.
S.TRANSL: Lienhuy他們出生的時候。
The day when Lienhuy and others were born.

S.FORM: qaytisan qaya wanayka.
S.TRANSL: 也很可怕。
It was also scary.

S.FORM: azu stangi tu snazau ti ya iza lepawta si mai QAniz ti ita mpapatay azu stangi ta.
S.TRANSL: (如果當時的房子)像現在的房子，我們不都全部死光了?
If the houses at that time were like those nowadays, wouldn’t we all have died?

S.FORM: Raya ti ya utuz tayan tazumaan lawlaw si mai QAniz ti ita m papatay.
S.TRANSL: 地震這麼大，在其他國家的話，我們不都全部死光了?
The earthquake was so strong. If we had been in other countries, wouldn’t we have all died?

S.FORM: mana paRin pama nani yau a yau a izaanna zuma na sunisna ni tama tiquli kwa tisiyang.
S.TRANSL: 其他,像Quli長輩的小孩Siyang。
Others, like Quli’s child, Siyang.

S.FORM: m.
S.TRANSL: 嗯。
Yes.

S.FORM: patuanna na izaanna snizas en ya.
S.TRANSL: 被屋頂壓到。
They were knocked down by the (falling) roof.

S.FORM: msuRaw ay paRin u snizas.
S.TRANSL: 倒下的木頭或屋頂。
The wood or the roof which collapsed.

S.FORM: snizas tu kawala zinna zana zipun ay kwa.
S.TRANSL: 屋頂是日本式瓦片。
The roof is made of Japanese tiles.

S.FORM: yau mRa iza kimsal ya tamana.
S.TRANSL: 他爸爸當警察。
His father was a policeman.

S.FORM: yau tayan iza qaniyau qmuzuan nani.
S.TRANSL: 他們居住在那裡。
They lived there.

S.FORM: nayau a Raya utuz nani.
S.TRANSL: 地震那麼大。
The earthquake was so strong.

S.FORM: paRaRiw pa ita zinna.
S.TRANSL: 我們要跑走，他們說。
We had to run away, they said.

S.FORM: Raya utuz maytis saiza tateRaq nani.
S.TRANSL: 地震很大，他們在裡面應該很害怕。
The earthquake was strong. They might be scared inside.

S.FORM: zamanna ti na iza ya na kawala.
S.TRANSL: ... the tiles.

S.FORM: ‘nay na kawala mlaqid ay tu zapanna.
S.TRANSL: 瓦片打到他的腳。
The tiles hit his feet.

S.FORM: mtenes mnaz ya izaanna.
S.TRANSL: 受傷了很久。
The wound failed to heal for a long time.

S.FORM: anu tisiciang.
S.TRANSL: (是) Siciang。
(That’s) Siciang.

S.FORM: tisiciang?.
S.TRANSL: Siciang?
Siciang?

S.FORM: suani ni kyuku ‘nay ya qademaanku ‘nay ya iza.
S.TRANSL: 她是kyuku的妹妹，我只記得那樣。
I could only remember that she was Kyuku’s sister.

S.FORM: zuma nani tangi ay ti tasaw.
S.TRANSL: 而且，今年。
In addition, this year...

S.FORM: ‘nay wama ‘nay haw tasaw Raya ay.
S.TRANSL: 只有那一年最大。
(The earthquake) was the strongest in that year.

S.FORM: yau pama Raya Raya ay wanaika mai tu iza u miza ya lazing.
S.TRANSL: 還有更大的，只是海沒有怎麼樣。
There was a stronger earthquake. But nothing happened to the sea.

S.FORM: wama utuz ‘nay ya.
S.TRANSL: 只有地震而已。
There was only trembling.

S.FORM: mai meted a lazing haw.
S.TRANSL: 海沒有退去。
The sea did not ebb.

S.FORM: m mai meted.
S.TRANSL: 對，(海)沒有退去。
Yes, (the sea) did not ebb.

S.FORM: nani nas e utani tasaw ‘nay mana yau pama ti iza ka tamaniq tipiwi.
S.TRANSL: 不知道民國幾年，我們的爸爸piwi還在(的時候)。
In the year ... (when) our father Piwi was still alive.

S.FORM: upitu ti ya mutuz m upitu ti naqiyan tasaw zinku yu.
S.TRANSL: 他70多歲了。
He was in his seventies.

S.FORM: upitubeting yau lima.
S.TRANSL: 七十五。
Seventy-five.

S.FORM: mana Raynguanku ka wama sanshijiu nian ‘nay supaRanku yu .
S.TRANSL: 我不知道，我只記得39年。

S.FORM: yau Raya utuz ‘nay.
S.TRANSL: 有大地震。
There was a strong earthquake.

S.FORM: Raya dames ‘nay utuzanna wanay tu mai mai.
S.TRANSL: 那個地震特別大。
That earthquake was particularly strong.

S.FORM: msuRaw ay tialiung kwa tu mqa qaytun.
S.TRANSL: 有一次大地震，是Aliung騎車跌倒的時候。
There was a strong earthquake when Aliung fell over while riding a bike.

S.FORM: wiya ti imi takalinkuan paising.
S.TRANSL: 我們到花蓮去看醫生。
We went to Hualien to see a doctor.

S.FORM: tiaki usa.
S.TRANSL: 不是。
No.

S.FORM: muna ay aizipna ta qani iza ay mpatay ay sunisna ni iza zinku yu ni siyu.
S.TRANSL: 在那個Siyu的小孩死掉之前。
(Before) Siyu’s child died,

S.FORM: walu upitubeting yau usiwa tasaw ‘nay.
S.TRANSL: 那時是79年。
It was Year 79 then.

S.FORM: nani tangi Rabeting mai Rabeting fenan haw yau uman yau ti.
S.TRANSL: 不到十分鐘又來。
(It) happened again within ten minutes.

S.FORM: anu ngid qautuz.
S.TRANSL: 如果地震要發生。
If the earthquake was to happen, ...

S.FORM: nani ‘nay mutuz tu nizanna ti qmudat ya naung na iza na.
S.TRANSL: 那個(地震)把全部的山的皮都削的光禿禿的。
Something (an earthquake) peeled all the mountains.

S.FORM: na niana maluung.
S.TRANSL: Maluung。
Maluung.

S.FORM: usa wi tazaya ka.
S.TRANSL: 不是，是在西邊。
No, it was in the west.

S.FORM: a tangi ti unay.
S.TRANSL: 那是最近的事。
That happened recently.

S.FORM: damu damu na qanilabay ya.
S.TRANSL: 就是Labay他們的村落啊。
It happened in Labay’s village.

S.FORM: tangi ti ya unay.
S.TRANSL: 那是最近的事。
That happened recently.

S.FORM: nizanna ti qmudat.
S.TRANSL: 全部都被剝光光了。
It’s all peeled off.

S.FORM: e walu betin yau uspat pama tasaw si .
S.TRANSL: 那才是八十四年的事情而已。

S.FORM: nikutkutanna ti ya e usiwa betin si .
S.TRANSL: 九十年。

S.FORM: naung na nantou kwa.
S.TRANSL: 南投的山都被挖空了。
The mountains of Nantou were scraped out.

S.FORM: usiwa betin .
S.TRANSL: 九十年。

S.FORM: n.
S.TRANSL: 嗯。
Yes.

S.FORM: usiwabetin ti u uwalubetin yau unem ‘nay mkudkudat tu nayau zana.
S.TRANSL: 那個是九十年，還是八十六年，(整個山)都被剝了皮。
That was Year 90 or 86 when it was peeled off.

S.FORM: nazau a melanayna ya nazau sim azu mniniR zinna tu pasababaw zinna kwa ya niutuzanna.
S.TRANSL: 聽說那地震，地就像篩子往上搖晃一樣。
It’s said that the earth was shaking up and down, as if it were sieved.

S.FORM: yau en ya.
S.TRANSL: 沒錯。
That’s right.

S.FORM: yau zuma pameng snazau mniniR tmayta stangi yau zuma ay snazau maqbabaw qmiwiqiwan zinna.
S.TRANSL: 聽說有的時候突然像篩子那樣左右搖，之後是上下搖動。
Some earthquakes shake horizontally, which looks like shaking a sieve, and then shake vertically, said they.

S.FORM: sngatanna haw.
S.TRANSL: (山)就被抬了起來。
(The mountain) was lifted up.

S.FORM: sngatanna tay pa ya manay na lazanna kwa wi ibabaw luqemu a.
S.TRANSL: 就被抬起來了，你看那山路啊，越來越高，都突出來了。
It’s lifted up. You see, the roads were getting higher and higher and sticked out.

S.FORM: e mai matiw imi ay haw.
S.TRANSL: 我們沒有去的時候。
When we didn’t go,

S.FORM: qesazui nani mai kutkutanna azuanna ti sataytaytaanna qusazui na sunis zinna.
S.TRANSL: 後來，人們並不去處理(地震後的山)，好像是要讓後代的子孫看，(以作為警惕)。
Afterwards, people did not clear up (the mountain), and it seemed that they wanted the younger generations to see it so that they would be vigilant.

S.FORM: pamenganna ti.
S.TRANSL: 只好那樣了。
That’s the only way.

S.FORM: king qusazui panganna ti nayau ya.
S.TRANSL: 就放著不理它。
They left (the mountain) as it was.

S.FORM: mai padasidasanna.
S.TRANSL: 沒有使它弄平。
They didn’t level it.

S.FORM: kinganna ti satitaanna na.
S.TRANSL: 就放著不動，好讓人家看。
They didn’t remove (the rocks and stones of the mountains) since they wanted them to be seen.

S.FORM: azu ti snayau si yu ‘nay zau yau a ibabaw naungta nani nayau.
S.TRANSL: 如果那樣的話，我們的山就會有高有低。
If that’s the case, our moutains would be high here and low there.

S.FORM: en ya.
S.TRANSL: 沒錯。
That’s right.

S.FORM: nayau snaqay na zau utuz haw en.
S.TRANSL: 地震的痕跡就是那樣。
That’s the traces of earthquakes.

S.FORM: smanu baiku tipayciq masang haw matiatiw iku sa quRu m ta takemuan nani.
S.TRANSL: 我祖母Payciq從前說：「我常常去磯碕的龜庵」。
My grandma Payciq once said, ‘I used to go to Taqemu in QuRu.’

S.FORM: ‘nay izaanna kwa damu na na takemu ‘nay kwa haw mana mwaza buRang zinta snayau.
S.TRANSL: 為什麼龜庵村落那裡的石頭那麼多?
Why were there so many rocks in the village of Takemu?

S.FORM: masang ma niutuzan zinna.
S.TRANSL: (因為)以前地震過。
(Because of) the earthquake in the past.

S.FORM: e nikunku na baibudan masang zinna.
S.TRANSL: 女生老人家以前說的故事。
The story told by female elders in the past.

S.FORM: qusazui haw damu zau haw qayau ma qasaqeRisan uman na nazau na utuz m zin na bai ti pay pay payciq nani.
S.TRANSL: Payciq老人家說以後這個村落還是會被地震侵襲(lit.被地震刮)。
This village might be stricken by an earthquake again, said an elder, Payciq.

S.FORM: nazau a qizuazuanniq zau tangayauanniq yau buRang kwa.
S.TRANSL: 我們居住的地方，前面有大石頭。
There are huge rocks in front of our residence.

S.FORM: pelulumana tu ‘nay salitun ay tamun.
S.TRANSL: 種有刺的菜的地方。
The place where vegetables with thorns are planted.

S.FORM: Rana.
S.TRANSL: 石頭花。
Flowers.

S.FORM: en ya.
S.TRANSL: 對，沒錯。
Yes, that’s right.

S.FORM: ni utuzan zau lepaw a zau da damu a zau masang.
S.TRANSL: 這村落以前地震過。
This village was once hit by an earthquake.

S.FORM: zin na baqbaqi zinna nani.
S.TRANSL: 老人們這樣說(指一代傳一代)。
The elders said so.

S.FORM: qumni wanay si qawman tizitayau siyu ala ta iza zau.
S.TRANSL: 不知何時，一定會變那樣，帶走這個...
(I’m) not sure when this will happen, but the situation would definitely become like that: Something will be taken away.

S.FORM: qayau ma siyu zinta yu qayau ma siyu nayau sansanuna paliway tu iza ya tu tu lazat tangi ya zuma lazat.
S.TRANSL: 可能會有這樣(的災難)，換新的人。
There might be such a catastrophe. People would be replaced.

S.FORM: nayau ti ya.
S.TRANSL: 就是那樣。
That’s it.

S.FORM: snayau a iza ya sa saquluanna timaita tu sa qusazui lazat usa.
S.TRANSL: (老天)要養我們，(所以才留下孩子)，一代傳一代，不是嗎?
(God) is to breed us (so that) there will be new generations, isn’t it?

S.FORM: yau ya sunis a yau utani.
S.TRANSL: 那個小孩幾歲?
How old is that child?

S.FORM: Rabetin tasawna yu.
S.TRANSL: 十歲。
Ten years old.

S.FORM: na Runanay ‘nay ta izauanna na niizauanna na.
S.TRANSL: 被留下的男孩子。
The boy who was left behind (in an earthquake).

S.FORM: azu niizauan na penasaqay ya.
S.TRANSL: 天災留下來的(小孩)。
Those (children who were) left behind in a natural disaster.

S.FORM: en ya tu Rabetin yau ulima dedan mai mnanum mai qman.
S.TRANSL: 15天，沒喝水，沒吃東西。
(He) did not drink and eat anything for 15 days.

S.FORM: mana binus.
S.TRANSL: 為什麼還活著?
Why was he still alive?

S.FORM: ‘nay ya nayau qaRupakunkuan.
S.TRANSL: 變成我們講故事的話題。
(This) becomes the topic of our stories.

S.FORM: nayau utuz sa izaan tu qaRulauz m sasikawmaan zau kya.
S.TRANSL: 這是我們所講的結尾。
This is the end of what we talked about.

S.FORM: mai nayau si taRnita azu tangi ay tu na zana masang ay si.
S.TRANSL: 如果不是這樣的話，我們怎麼知道像現在和以前的事情?
If it weren’t like that, how could we know the things in the present and in the past?

S.FORM: taRnisu tu snazau snazau zin na baibudan.
S.TRANSL: 你怎麼會知道老人家說什麼什麼的。
How could you know what the elders said?

S.FORM: wanay ma ya si sikawmaan zata ya iza kiya.
S.TRANSL: 我們只有口述的而已。
We only have oral narrations.

S.FORM: zana busus saiza zau izaanna qawman yau a sudad.
S.TRANSL: 那個閩南人的話，一定有記載。
People of Southern Min must have written records.

S.FORM: mai ita tu sudad.
S.TRANSL: 我們沒有記載。
We don’t have written records.

S.FORM: wama maq ngibiR na baqbaqi.
S.TRANSL: 只有從老人家那裡口述的。
Only the oral narrations from the elders.

S.FORM: en maitusudad ita haw.
S.TRANSL: 對，我們沒有記載。
That’s right. We don’t have written records.

S.FORM: en.
S.TRANSL: 對。
That’s right.

S.FORM: azu qanibanday azu qanibanday yau tmayta tu sudad.
S.TRANSL: 像偕萬來他們，有看書。
People such as Banday read books.

S.FORM: yau singsina haw wanay sa.
S.TRANSL: 他們有老師。
They have teachers.

S.FORM: ‘nay si zata ti ay tangi ay ti iza kiya nisangian na senangi na busus.
S.TRANSL: 閩南人做的。
The things done by the people of Southern Min.

S.FORM: en ya nisangina ti ni yesu ‘nay en ya na seisiw.
S.TRANSL: 耶穌做的事。
The thing done by Jesus.

S.FORM: en ya.
S.TRANSL: 對。
Yes.

S.FORM: zana ‘nay nani zana ti na iza maq zuma ay lawlaw a.
S.TRANSL: 從別的國家記載的。
From the written records of other countries.

S.FORM: senangina ti aita makezaq ay kebalan niana ya.
S.TRANSL: 我們真正的噶瑪蘭有什麼呢?
What do we genuine Kavalan have?

S.FORM: yau ay ti zata ay.
S.TRANSL: 我們有什麼呢?
What do we have?

S.FORM: yau ay sudad.
S.TRANSL: 有書的。
We had written records.

S.FORM: yau zinna masang qaya nani.
S.TRANSL: 他們說以前也有。
They said that there were (written records) in the past.

S.FORM: smanu baqiku nani sapaR zana busus zinna azu ti tangi sapaR ya.
S.TRANSL: 我的祖父說閩南人用木板(來寫)，很像現在的木板。
My grandpa said that the Min people used wooden boards (for writing), much like the boards nowadays.

S.FORM: azu sapaR mlinamaw mlinamaw.
S.TRANSL: 木板會浮。
Wooden boards are able to float.

S.FORM: aita kebalan nani tu sapaqunianna sapaR.
S.TRANSL: 我們噶瑪蘭人要木板做什麼？
What could we Kavalan people do with wooden boards?

S.FORM: qakikya.
S.TRANSL: 很快。
Soon.

S.FORM: qakikya ma mdiyuq qangi ma betu zinna zinna kwa.
S.TRANSL: (木板)很快就爛了，所以他們說石頭比較好。
(Wooden boards) decay soon, so they said stones were better.

S.FORM: mai qadiyuq betu ‘nay.
S.TRANSL: 石頭不會腐爛。
Stones do not decay.

S.FORM: sudadanna ti tu betu nani.
S.TRANSL: 於是他們(噶瑪蘭人)就寫在石頭上。
So they (the Kavalan people) wrote on stones.

S.FORM: simpaska ti.
S.TRANSL: (他們[噶瑪蘭人和閩南人])互相比較。
(They [the Kavalan people and the Southern Min people]) compared with each other.

S.FORM: paska pa ita ya tiana ya qamayni ya qatenes zinna ya.
S.TRANSL: 他們就說：「我們來比較一下吧，看誰，那一個(木板或石頭)會保存的比較久。」
They said, ‘Let’s make a comparison to see who ... which (either boards or stones) will be more durable.’

S.FORM: timu timu msanem a busus nani.
S.TRANSL: 結果反而是閩南人比較聰明。
It turned out that the Southern Min people were smarter.

S.FORM: qayzi pa ita ta zanzanuman zinna tu palinamawakita.
S.TRANSL: 他們說：「我們要到水中去，讓木板和石頭在水中浮起來。」
They said, ‘Let’s go to the water and make (boards and stones) float on the water.’

S.FORM: Rayngu ti zata kebalan tu qalinamawan.
S.TRANSL: 我們噶瑪蘭的(石頭)浮不起來。
Our Kavalan’s (stones) cannot float.

S.FORM: wiya ti m mlinemnem mai ti ya sudadta ya aita na mai ti ya kebalan zinna kwa aita.
S.TRANSL: (我們的石頭)就沉了下去，我們噶瑪蘭的文字就因此沒了，他們這麼說。
(Stones) sank, and thus our Kavalan writing was gone, said they.

S.FORM: taRnina tu msanem busus.
S.TRANSL: 他們怎麼會知道(閩南人)這麼聰明?
How could they know the Southern Min people were so smart?

S.FORM: aita ya atu kizaya yuenzhumin.
S.TRANSL: 我們和阿美族，這些原住民(都沒有自己的文字)。
We and the Amis people, the aborigines (do not have our own writing).

S.FORM: qawman qasuqas a betu a zau zinna sayza na busus.
S.TRANSL: 閩南人也許想說石頭一定會輸的。
The Southern Min people probably thought that stones would definitely lose.

S.FORM: en ya.
S.TRANSL: 是啊。
That’s right.

S.FORM: baksiwanna tazanzanuman.
S.TRANSL: (石頭)被丟到水裡。
(The stones) were thrown into the water.

S.FORM: nayau ya yau taanemanta ya sudadta zinna nayau sasikawmaan na baqian.
S.TRANSL: 祖先們從前常說我們的文字保存在我們的心中。
Our ancestors used to say that our writing exists in our hearts.

S.FORM: mlinemnem.
S.TRANSL: (我們的文字)沈下去了。
(Our writing) sank into the water.

S.FORM: en ya.
S.TRANSL: 沒錯。
That’s right.

S.FORM: wiya ti taizaan a sudadta ta zanzanuman si.
S.TRANSL: 我們的文字沉進去那個，那個水裡面去了。
Our writing sank down into somewhere, into the water.

S.FORM: taRnita mkala.
S.TRANSL: 我們怎麼找的回來！
How could we find it?

S.FORM: anu supaRan na kebalan tu qa linemnem a betu nani.
S.TRANSL: 如果噶瑪蘭人知道石頭會沉下去的話，(就不會拿石頭跟閩南人的木板比了)。
If Kavalan people had known stones would sink, (they would not have compared their stones with Southern Min people’s boards.)

S.FORM: taRnina tu ngid palinamaw busus.
S.TRANSL: 不過他們怎麼會知道閩南人是要讓(東西)浮起來。
(But) how could they have known that Southern Min people wanted to make (them) float?

S.FORM: en ya msanem busus ya.
S.TRANSL: 對啊，閩南人很聰明。
Yes, Southern Min people were smart.

S.FORM: anu tanemanna haw qa suqas busus kaya zinna.
S.TRANSL: 如果當初是把他們埋起來的話，閩南人就會輸了。
If (they) had been buried instead, Southern Min people would have lost.

S.FORM: en ya.
S.TRANSL: 對啊。
Yes.

S.FORM: nianu qalatuq na betu.
S.TRANSL: 石頭怎麼會腐蝕呢？
How could stones possibly become rusty?

S.FORM: yau ‘nay si uRiR na kebalan ma izaan msanem a busus.
S.TRANSL: 噶瑪蘭人太愚蠢了，而閩南人太聰明了。
Kavalan people were stupid while Southern Min people were smart.

S.FORM: tanemakita zinna sayza na kebalan.
S.TRANSL: 噶瑪蘭人可能以為是要把他們(指石頭)埋起來。
Kavalan people probably thought, ‘Let’s bury (them).’

S.FORM: timangu aita ya msanem a busus.
S.TRANSL: 結果卻是閩南人較聰明。
It turned out that Southern Min people were smart.

S.FORM: mai qaliyuq a betu zinna sayza.
S.TRANSL: 我們噶瑪蘭人可能想說石頭不會腐爛。
Kavalan people probably thought that stones would not decay.

S.FORM: timangu sanem a busus.
S.TRANSL: 結果卻是閩南人較聰明。
It turned out that Southern Min people were smart.

S.FORM: mlinemnem ti.
S.TRANSL: (石頭)沉了。
(The stones) sank.

S.FORM: en ya nayau a aita ya masang na mRaymazuq a baqbaqita.
S.TRANSL: 對啊，我們以前的祖先就是那樣笨。
Yes, our ancestors were stupid in that way.

S.FORM: naquniakita.
S.TRANSL: 我們該怎麼辦呢？
What should we do?

S.FORM: zinta na mai qaya yu mRaymazuq.
S.TRANSL: 不過話又說回來了，(噶瑪蘭人)也不太笨啦。
But on the other hand, (Kavalan people) are not that stupid.

S.FORM: nianu nianu peliya saiza nayau ti qaya tangi zinta mai inaRanna na Raya ya nanganta kebadan si.
S.TRANSL: 還好，政府把我們噶瑪蘭正名了(把我們噶瑪蘭的名字叫醒了)。
Luckily, the government rectified the name of our tribe, Kavalan.

S.FORM: mai ti ita yu.
S.TRANSL: (否則)我們就完了。
(Otherwise), we were over.

S.FORM: qawman ti zau sikawmaanta mai ti mqamai kya haw.
S.TRANSL: 就是如此，我們講的話才不會遺失。
That’s it. Our language won’t disppear.

S.FORM: anu mai ita nazauanna na ‘daq tmaqsi ay nani qawman mai ti mai ti mai ita kasianem pa nayau zukat tu nanganta.
S.TRANSL: 如果沒有那些讀書的人，我們就不會想到要正名(把我們的名字弄出來)。
If it were not for those who studied, we would not have thought about re-designating our tribe.

S.FORM: m azu nazau ti ya aita ya nayau ti ya baibaita mRaymazuq.
S.TRANSL: 就是那樣，我們的祖先真傻。
That’s it. Our ancestors are foolish.

S.FORM: pe peliya mai yau snazau ay ngid sinanam haw.
S.TRANSL: 還好有這些人在學習研究。
Luckily, there are those who are studying or doing research.

S.FORM: aita ya zau a baibudan mRayzu mRaymazuq zin ta.
S.TRANSL: 我們說這些老人家很傻，嗯?
We said these elders were foolish, right?

S.FORM: nazau a sunista kyami wiya ti nayau nazau a kelawkaway tazumaan damu nani.
S.TRANSL: 我們的小孩去別的村落工作。
Our children go to other villages for work.

S.FORM: matatapun ti tu iza si tu busus saiza niana si.
S.TRANSL: 跟閩南人在一起。
They are with people of Southern Min.

S.FORM: niana qasikawmaanna.
S.TRANSL: 他們講什麼話?
What (language) do they speak?

S.FORM: tmaqsi.
S.TRANSL: 讀書的時候呢?
What about while (they are) studying?

S.FORM: niz yau ta izaan si ta bubususan si.
S.TRANSL: 全部都在閩南人生活的地方。
All (of them) are at the places of people of Southern Min.

S.FORM: niana ya iza kebadan tmanan Rabi ti.
S.TRANSL: 噶瑪蘭人回來時都很晚了。
(When) Kavalan people return, it is late at night.

S.FORM: mai uta ni ya anu anu sunista nazau a sinanam tu sikawmaanta nani qangi pama qaya haw.
S.TRANSL: 如果我們的孩子學母語應該比較好。
If our children learn mother tongue, it would be better.

S.FORM: mana iwaliu ti ya.
S.TRANSL: 為什麼反而變成(是別人在學我們的語言)?
How come (it is people other than Kavalan who are learning our language) instead?

S.FORM: Raymazuq ita mai ita tu sa pataqsian tu sunista si.
S.TRANSL: 我們沒有能力，不能讓我們的小孩去讀書。
We are unable to have our children study. (We don’t have what can have our children study, i.e. money.)

S.FORM: zana ‘daq nani satuRin.
S.TRANSL: 別人的話，很有辦法。
Others are able to (do so).

S.FORM: qamamai tu kelisiwna pawRat.
S.TRANSL: 沒有錢，還是想辦法。
(Even though) we don’t have money, we still manage (to have our children study).

S.FORM: azu ti zau baiku ya taqsika nianu zinsu o.
S.TRANSL: 就像我的孫子，我叫他讀書，你說他怎麼回答呢?
Like my grandson, I told him to study. You know what he said?

S.FORM: daytan pama iku si yau pa iku sapunaka pama kelisiw tu sataqsianku.
S.TRANSL: 我先賺錢，我先把錢存起來，再去讀書。
I make and save money first before going to school.

S.FORM: tu snazau ti qaya ya nani ya tu mbiRes ti Rayngu ti tuzus bai zau si tu tangi Rayngu ti.
S.TRANSL: 他跛腳了，沒有辦法(去讀書)了，一直到現在。
He is lame and cannot (study) till now.

S.FORM: en ya.
S.TRANSL: 沒錯。
That’s right.

S.FORM: en ya.
S.TRANSL: 沒錯。
That’s right.

S.FORM: qangi pangaRaz ita si qumni si nayau a izaanna snayau sepianna tu snazau tu waway a zau.
S.TRANSL: 我們只好這個樣子，依賴命運的安排。
We can only defer to the arrangements of destiny.

S.FORM: mRaymanges kasianem.
S.TRANSL: (因為)不知道怎麼辦。
(Because) we don’t know what to do.

S.FORM: bai zana baqina tu kelawkawayanna snayau 1mai1 nayau mai.
S.TRANSL: 他的阿公給他工作，還是沒用。
His grandpa gave him work, but in vain.

S.FORM: nayauakita kunkuta naRin.
S.TRANSL: 我們的故事講到這裡，可不可以?
That’s all our story.

S.FORM: en ya.
S.TRANSL: 好。
Okay.

S.FORM: nayau ti saizi anu pun ti tu utuz nani nengi ti ya.
S.TRANSL: 講完地震的故事就好了。
(As long as) (we) finished the story about the earthquake, that’s okay.

S.FORM: pun ti tu utuz n pakunku nani mai ti nengi ti ya nengi a.
S.TRANSL: 講完地震的故事就好了。
(As long as) (we) have finished the story about the earthquake, that’s okay.

S.FORM: en ya.
S.TRANSL: 對。
That’s right.

S.FORM: en ya.
S.TRANSL: 對。
That’s right.

S.FORM: tinu pakunku tu snazau ay .
S.TRANSL: 誰講這種故事?
Who told this kind of story?
